channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? — 54 replies — 216.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet?

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-06-02 12:48 UTC

OP

Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? Allow me to explain.
Cryptocurrencies support decentralized networks, which are used for data computation and storage. These networks are cheaper to use than traditional server infrastructure.
If it's cheaper, why don't corporations use them? One, there are regulations preventing adoption, but CLARITY will change this in the US. Two, corporations have invested trillions into building and maintaining server infrastructure. Liquidation is risky from there point of view because they don't understand decentralized networks. A few brave corporations will lead this great transition, and when they are successful, everyone will follow.
You are in the final window of being early. Don't fuck this up.

Early replies (first 3)

62292772
>>62292765
cope

62292786
>>62292765
How much does 1 Gigabyte on the Bitcoin Blockchain cost?

62292794
>>62292786
>thinks a blockchain is the only data structure for distributed storage

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62296638
>>62292765
It doesnt offer a solution to a problem that exists.

62297373
>>62296629
For a huge amount of the business world, actual compute isn't even the real issue. It's interconnects. Data movement. When you're breaking up a 10 terabyte job across a thousand nodes, you are very very rarely bottlenecked by compute. You're bottlenecked by shuffling the data between machines. The whole reason massive datacenters exist is because they can use insane interconnects (1TB, even 10 TB/s fabric). Using retarded internet shitcoin routing mechanisms to attempt this is like using a garden hose to put out a lava flow.

62297920
>>62296638
t. isn't in business and has no money

62297957
>>62296032
i think your missing the point of the tech

62298066
>>62296629
>>62297373
There are P2P relay schemes for efficient data flow. Kademlia routing is one example.

62298770
>>62292765
It has been adopted. Not on a grand scale though. Bitcoin is a deflationary store of value. XMR to buy drugs on the darknet. Stablecoins are used. Perpdex as an online gambling platforms.

62301398
>>62295608
>2028? more like 2030 if the hype bubble pops first

62301403
>>62297373
>implying some shitcoin meshnet can replace infiniband

lmao, anon doesn't understand why dc exist

62301409
>>62297957
the main thing missing here is the practical tradeoff, not the headline claim

62302054
>>62297957
Anyone invoking "the tech" is almost certainly stupid. There is exactly one piece of useful tech in crypto:
- the idea of a distributed ledger than is extremely hard to forge or alter

That's it. Everything else, which at this point is about 95% of the gibberish coming from your scamming overlords is pure nonsense. Bait to keep you on the hook because it makes you feel smart and in-the-know.

Just think about the whole idea of a "trusted oracle". How the fuck is a trusted oracle going to provide some techno-based unbiased ground truth for "this company failed to complete grouting my bathroom properly"? I'll give you a hint: there's huge huge companies that already do this. They're called insurance companies with vast numbers of field employees who investigate insurance claims. And millions of small-claims lawyers. There's no way to do it other than physical inspection. An infallible "oracle" is absolute nonsense for probably 99% of all 'contracts' in the world.

62302089
>>62302054
Holy shit, you're dumb.

62302097
>>62302089
The thing about you crypto fags is that you never actually attempt to rebut anything. You just instantly go to the boomer-esque insults with nothing else. It's like any boomer politics forum, but for mutt zoomers and failson millenials.

62302126
>>62302097
Here's some rebuttal for you.
A ledger is one of many applications for distributed networks.
You don't understand what oracles are. You understand what some YouTuber without a computer science background said about oracles.

62302152
>>62302126
An oracle is a trusted service that can provide the inputs needed to satisfy a smart contract, no? The question becomes how exactly it can verify these inputs for anything other than very large scale questions, like the standard hurr-durr rainfall/crop-insurance example.

t. computer scientist btw

62302300
>>62292765
> If it's cheaper, why don't corporations use them?

Because corporations want power and control over things. They want to centralize it in their hands.

62302324
>>62302300
Stupid. Cloud compute is an enormous business. AWS, GCP, etc. Companies use them because it's cheaper than setting up their own hardware these days. COST is the key. If your stupid shitcoin mesh compute network was anywhere near as cost effective they'd be using it.

62302421
>>62302324
AWS/GCP isnt decentralized. It has authority that controls it.

62302431
>>62302421
Sure, but you said "corporations want power and control over things". The number of corporations who USE cloud compute is 10000x higher than the number of corporations who provide cloud compute. So, clearly, those 10000x corporations don't really care about control. If they did, they'd have their own hardware, right? They just want to run some ad-analysis jobs as cheaply as possible. So they choose the cloud providers, who have assembled insanely powerful clusters that are easy to use.

62302440
>>62302431
And just so you rubes have some sort of idea of the scale of "big data", here's an article about Amazon's S3 service that used to send physical trucks with 100 PETABYTE capacity to places that needed to upload their shit to AWS.

https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/aws-retires-snowmobile-truck-based-data-transfer-service/

And they RETIRED that shit because it wasn't even enough!. Your duplo block shitcoin mesh compute scheme isn't even a fart in the wind compared to this.

62302497
>>62302054
>>62302152
You're conflating "inputting" data into smart contracts with actually sourcing data.
Oracles are very rarely the data source itself, they merely take the data source selected by a user and put it on chain securely.

>muh bathroom grout
Who the fuck cares what data the user wants, if they want bathroom grout data then that's what they want and they will have to pay for it. People want strange shit sometimes.

62302543
>>62292794
Then show the costs of maintaining non-blockchain decentralized network vs just maintaining your own server. Oh wait you can't because security costs of something with so much open volatility are impossible to predict.

62302568
>>62296032
It could be cheaper for the companies themselves who offload all their hardware costs and energy demands onto idealistic cucks like the anon you are arguing with, but the main problem is security.

62302620
>>62298066
The relay schemes would all be exponentially more energy and time efficient (not to mention secure) if the data was all flowing within the same large data center rather than all around the world through a bunch of different networks.

62302631
The main reason is that it doesn't improve the existing systems enough and is still far too clunky with a poor user experience for almost all applications of the technology.

That, with the lack of regulatory clarity, are the two biggest factors.

62302876
>>62302543
Check out Swarm. It's the Ethereum Foundation's data storage network.

62302882
>>62302620
no

62302939
>>62302876
Why would I if you have checked it out and still can't actually answer the question?

>>62302882
Yes, you can't make long distance more efficient transfer than short distance with all else being equal.

62302969
>>62302939
I did answer your question.
Also, clients receive data from servers in packets so there's little difference between receiving data chunks from one location compared to multiple locations, especially since distributed storage networks have bandwidth requirements to run a node. I use Swarm, and I don't notice a difference in speed.

62302990
>>62302969
No, if you are >>62302876, you told me to go check somewhere else that might answer my question.
>there's little difference between receiving data chunks from one location compared to multiple locations
Except the time it takes to send the data over the distance which really adds up when sending a lot of data.

62303089
>>62302990
Swarm and other distributed storage networks send data in parallel. It's quite fast. I haven't done a speed test, but again, I haven't had a slower experience.

item.title: /smg/ -CLF tranny edition — 51 replies — 204.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ -CLF tranny edition

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-06-02 12:48 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62302797
AI doesn't care about oil shortage, just water to power the electric generators? that's why we keep pumping uncontrollably? my port is 99% tech.

62302802
>futures

62302805
I DIDNT BUY. FUCK ME

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302984
smg is strangely quiet right now...

62302998
>>62302954
it gives credibility to the thread

62303005
>>62302973
No but XFAB is his new pick and is about to run

KEK OIL BAGGOTS
KEK BTC BAGGOTS
KEK METALS BAGGOTS

62303009
>>62302984
All the autism is refreshing news till cotp schedules drop

62303013
>>62303005
Sivers is still his main pick and still got some room to pump. XFAB is also a good pick

62303016
>>62302984
The bots are rebooting

62303020
HOOD NIGGAZ HOW WE HOLDING UP???
I MIGHT HAVE TO STEAL SOME MONEY OUT MY MOMMAS PURSE BUT ITS OKAY SOCIAL SECURITY JUST HIT WE GONE MAKE IT ANYWAYS

62303022
>>62302973
>>62303013
>30b mcap for 300m revenue
CLOWN
MARKET

62303023
me when I look at the astrocharts

62303029
IM SLURPING GOOGL.
THE OLD FART BUFFET BULLISH ON THEM.
AND THEY'RE THE SPEARHEAD OF AI.
LET'S FUCKING GOOO GOOOGL

62303031
spce just rugged...

62303035
>>62303031
kek

62303039
>>62303031
Time to slurp

62303041
>>62302984

62303050
>Sivers pumping 70% on one month old news
Is this some kind of top signal

62303051
can't believe you all are using a tranny thread whats wrong with you, you are all tranies now

62303055
I don't know what to buy in this clown market

62303056
I was promised that I could buy more DRAM today at a discount...

62303057
>>62303055
Rolls royce

62303058
>>62303031
kek baggie

62303061
>35 dollars per week allocated to a high risk portfolio
>140 allocated to low risk per week
This sure is slow but I'm waiting for the midterm recession caused by the israeli market fuckery

62303065
>>62303031
what is spce ?

62303066
>>62303055
Prior to the dot com bubble bursting, they got a retired trained chimpanzee to pick random stock tickers from a dartboard.
He outperformed like 90% of professional traders.
Pick something random.

62303073
>>62303055

62303075
EUR/USD sissies, seems like we're gonna need to hedge our USD stocks because the euro is going higher again...

62303080
>>62303073
why does this just look like a bunch of random stocks combined in a etf

62303083
>>62303050
this ain't old news bobotard, dropped this morning June 2 07:00 CET :

Sivers just announced a collab with GlobalFoundries their laser arrays going into GF's silicon photonics platform and SCALE™ CPO solutions. GFS is a major Nasdaq foundry, this is real AI infra exposure.

62303087
>>62303031
Kek SPCE baggots

>>62303055
GRRR under $20 at market open

62303091
>>62303031
>rugged

I just checked it and it's up 22% ?

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 37 replies — 148.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-02 12:48 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62299589
He's selling his high cost basis coins, the market is dumping and he's just going to buy them cheaper. Guys a fucking genius

62299647
>>62299589
Would have been even more genius if he hadn’t been seething against BTC when it was $200

62299824
>>62299187
hes so fucked, LUNA 2.0 inc

62299854
.

62299869
>>62299202
had to pay Sergey for ccip access

62299903
>>62299187
Cats out of the bag sandjeep

62299907
this idiot kills the market

62300165
>>62299187
dumb ass apparently buys over the counter and sells on a centralized exchange on the open market. controlled opposition nigger detected kek.

62300746
>>62299202
For some reason.

62300755
>>62299205
He can do whatever he wants tho

62300807
>>62299325
Yeah before but I don't mind

62300810
>ponzi scheme can't dilute on the back of a speculative asset anymore
Buttcoin is over.

62300991
>>62299187
This is how it starts dumbass, why sell BTC at all if he's got money? especially selling at a loss says alot.

62301317
>>62300807
I don't either, I have no skin in the game. Just funny how the poster child for btc is going back on his word.

62301382
The fact that crypto is even a thing shows how most people have zero understanding of money or value.

62301632
>>62301590
Absolute bullshit as usual.

Microsoft, Coca-Cola or Boeing don’t sell their intellectual property; it doesn’t mean this IP has no value, that it’s ignored by rating agencies, or that it’s not real.

The only person who suggested MSTR would never sell its BTC is Saylor himself; no one ever told him his BTC "can’t be sold" or prevented him from selling anything. He cornered himself into this position by his own volition.

And selling 32 BTC only proves that you had 32 BTC, nothing more. The only unquestionable method to prove your Bitcoin is "real" would be to give us the adresses of your wallets, Mike. The entire world could check the state of your BTC reserves in real time. But you never want to reveal this basic info. Why?

Lies lies and lies.

62301643
>>62301590
beta

62302272
>>62301317
I don't care who the major players are that sell their BTC. It doesn't really affect me since I'm focused on the long term. That's what I mean.

62302278
>>62299379
but every BTC holder can sell at will. That's not exactly unique.

62302621
>>62299533
He has a choice.

62302691
>>62299533
>buy 20k btc
>price drops .5%
>sell 32 btc
>price drops 5%
He just can’t win

62302729
>>62299202
The company is extremely opportunistic. At times it accumulated bitcoin using cash, at times it used common stock dilution, at times it used corporate bonds, and now it is using preferred stock. A dogma like "we will never sell ANY" bitcoin is unreasonable because:
- It isn't how the real world works. Anything on any company's balance sheet is subject to selling if the circumstances demand it. It is part of its creditworthiness, valuation, and so on
- It severely limits opportunities

Many months ago Strategy already acknowledged that it might sell bitcoin tactically (while staying long strategically) to:
- Pay back debt
- Pay dividends
- Buy back common stock if it becomes too undervalued

They stated the reason for the current sale during their last earnings call a month ago: the goal is to get the headline "Saylor is selling" out at a controlled point in time and price in the irrational panic from breaking the apparent taboo. Check out any forum, 4chan, reddit, twitter, comments are nearly 100% about "this ship is sinking, Saylor is broke, ponzi collapsing", because they only read the headline that Saylor is selling without bothering to look into why he's doing it.

62302766
>>62302691
He can if he sold earlier

62302807
>>62299187
thats like saying "oh satoshi only sold 32 btc"

of course its a big deal if this cucksaylor with $10billion of debt and 800,000 btc sells some

62302809
>>62302807
Saylor cuckold fanboys are too retarded to understand this. Imagine worshipping a boomer faggot from the dotcom bubble who spent 6 years buying "dips" and is now down -5 billion dollars kek

62302811
>>62302809
the sentiment is obvious outside of delusional saylormaxis, maybe not soon but eventually this house of cards topples

62302815
>>62302811
the scary part is how plenty of BTC maxis fell for his bullshit. MSTR is a giant ponzi

62303046
>SELL ORGANS KEEP BITCOIN NEVER SELL BITCOIN
>sells insanely small fraction of bitcoin
>see it's literally nothing, a drop in the ocean
>also he's just selling to prove he can for the bankers because such a small fraction provides confidence it's all truly worth that
retarded

62303090
>>62302809
>full stops instead of commas
you third worlders ruined this board

item.title: ATTENTION MARINES! — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ATTENTION MARINES!

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-02 12:49 UTC

OP

TradingView is spreading FUD about Chainlink. Stop using that fuddie website

Early replies (first 3)

62302863
>>62302856
You mean they are singling out a single asset and are incessantly posting negative price action for this asset only? That's crazy man

62302868
>>62302863
yeah they're probably paid by NEXO Bulgarians or Layerzero. We can't let them get away with this

62302905
>>62302863
I just checked, and they aren't even doing this? Everything on there is red not just Chainlink??

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302946
>>62302905
>expectng third world fuddies to make sense
cmon anon you're more retarded than OP for replying

62302995
>>62302946
You don't believe him anon? Just because he is a dysgenic brown jeet with a cuck fetish?? How close minded of you

62303037
>>62302856
>>62302905
>>62302946
oh I get it
when your or I post the price/chart it's fud but when a website tracks it it's just data

62303042
>>62303037
There are many pairings you could post, you could even zoom out to 2017 on those charts yet you only post LINK charts (after 2020 or 2021) why is that?

And as always never selling

62303067
>>62303037
If there was a website dedicated to posting negative price action for a single asset only, that would be considered FUD, yes.

62303084
>>62303042
because most link holders are underwater
the price action has sucked since its last ath
sure us marines are lucky we got in early
but you could just post an xrp chart if "up and to the right" is all you care about since xrp is up more than link from the beginning
>>62303067
does it really though? kek

62303085
>>62302963
>shitter's full

62303092
>>62303084
Why dont you look at 99.9% of coins made in 2017 and post those charts? What about ADA? Litecoin? What about XRP from 2017?

And again, im never selling

item.title: I have 46 BTC. They used to be the dominant asset in my portfolio, but now I feel like they're a time bomb. Should I sel… — 13 replies — 22.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have 46 BTC. They used to be the dominant asset in my portfolio, but now I feel like they're a time bomb. Should I sel…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-02 12:15 UTC

OP

I have 46 BTC. They used to be the dominant asset in my portfolio, but now I feel like they're a time bomb. Should I sell?

Early replies (first 3)

62296412
>>62296205
Send address so i can check on Blockchain

62296423
>>62296205

Buy Polkadot near all-time low desu

62296432
>>62296205

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298496
>>62296205
>Should I sell?
you can invest into my project if you would like.

1) platform like Steam, but you forever own your game (not a revokable licence), and much more perks/social for gamers. you can resell used game (but keeping it = more perks). On blockchain. (not a shitcoin, just mechanism to note ownership).

2) a game about hacking but actually realistic

sounds interesting?

0) level: get in on the ground floor (tm) - frens, anons, family, (((you))) possibly
1) level: small incubators (100-300$ k USD), small reserach grants if any. (using part of profit to pay up level 0 investors)
2) level: bigger investors, possibly IPO
3) focusing on profits from the end users.

62298534
>>62296205
Sell 20btc for the US30Y. That would balance it out perfectly.

62298594
>>62298496
We already discussed this in another thread, but the problem of your project is that you’re competing against billion-dollar companies with decades of existence, but you have almost no capital, no experience (?), no moat (blockchain ownership to resell games is not something *both* players and devs really desire), no existing customers and no network effect (the good developers won’t be selling here in the beginning, so it won’t take off).

Check how Garena did to become a major platform with a solid moat right away.

62298668
>>62296205
>1oz .999 Copper Coin
Thats as real as bitcoin will ever be, by the way, 1oz of copper is currently €0.36, Thats what a real bitcoin is actually worth.

62298833
>>62298668

62301495
>>62298594
>We already discussed this in another thread, but the problem of your project is that
>you’re competing against billion-dollar companies with decades of existence,
that's ok for me

>but you have almost no capital,
can do the programming work myself. marketing comes later, that's mainly for the first investors and next level of investor (but I believe in word of mouth as well)
>no experience (?)
programming/blockchain

>same no moat (blockchain ownership to resell games is not something *both* players and devs really desire),
>no existing customers and no network effect (the good developers won’t be selling here in the beginning, so it won’t take off).
it's for gamers who want real ownership of games, and game developers (indie) who are ok with providing that instead of predatory practices like EA and many others
not every game studio will want that, but that's fine

I was thinking of the problem - of game devels being "scared" of their game being re-sold.
1) this "problem" is not that big. yard sales were always possible in good old times of game CDs, and never caused some large issue for game developers. piracy alone caused far more
2) first person - buying it "brand new" will get extra perks;
3) you lose many perks when you sell the game - first of: you can no longer [legally] play it; you also don't receive updates. leaderboards, achievements, badges

other perks, for curren-owners include community (more open to current-owners) and various other "social" aspects. they do matter do some part of gamers.

62302922
>>62296205
Sell and buy Chainlink.

62302930
You have to be a retard to keep BTC when LINK is at 8.8

62302938
>>62296423
it is going down to 0.00001

62302997
>>62301495
>that's ok for me

If you want to self-annihilate feel free, but your chance of success is so low it requires scientific notation to express, so I'm gonna pass.

item.title: What's the future of XMR? — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's the future of XMR?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-02 12:40 UTC

OP

What's the future of XMR?

Early replies (first 3)

62302657
>>62302644
how does 1 dollar sound

62302667
>>62302644
How does dismantling the entire fucking state apparatus sound?

62302832
>>62302644
No matter the price, it'll get used by those who with to be free of the jewish yoke.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62303068
>>62302832
based
(((keynesians))) deserve the rope

item.title: When ICP goes to $1000 you will all cry and hate — 6 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
When ICP goes to $1000 you will all cry and hate

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-02 12:19 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62302706
>>62302692
not before 币安人生 币安人生 币安人生 gets in the top 10

62302790
>>62302706
…

62302933
>>62302692
I only have a couple hundred ICP I bought around $2.5-3 but id quit my job immediately

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302962
>>62302933
300 icp = $300,000

62302986
>>62302692
Sell me on it. What's the difference between this and every other shitcoin?

62303011
>>62302692
>When ICP goes to $1000 you will all cry and hate
The $1,000 Scenario isn't real. $1,000 per token, its market cap would have to shoot up to $553 billion, retard.... not going to happen.

item.title: I think a /biz/ group chat would be chill. — 13 replies — 11.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
I think a /biz/ group chat would be chill.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-02 11:43 UTC

OP

I think a /biz/ group chat would be chill. For discussion of our picks in stocks/crypto and so on. A manageable amount of people, like 20-100. Would be nice to avoid people having random arguments about nothing every other thread and the never ending LINK posters.

Only issue is keeping scammers and stupid people out of there and not sure which platform is fitting. Only rules: be nice. Billions must smile

Early replies (first 3)

62301815
sorry i dont speak hindi

62301849
>>62301815
Unironically thought about checking IP addresses to keep out scammers but Indians can just use VPNs so everyone would have to dox themselves which isn’t really all that great. Think there’s way less Indians here now that the board is so dead anyways

62301928
>>62301812
/biz/ is a chaat group

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62301935
>>62301928
It kind of is but a group chat is just a different social environment which I think could be nice

62301946
>>62301849
must post a white/asian hand with timestamp every hour or get kicked from the chat lmao.

62301954
>>62301946
We'll figure something out

>>62301938
Sounds amazing bro I bet I’ll find the next gem there

62301957
>>62301954
they found NOCK early...

62301969
>>62301957
Never heard of it desu. Taking a quick look at the chat it seems like the thing I would want to avoid, a lot of off topic bullshit so you would miss the next big thing if someone posted about it anyways

62302003
>>62301812
isn't that basically what coindashy is?

62302042
>>62302003
Tf is that

62302147
>>62302042
This website: https://coindashy.com/
has an anon chat and crypto charts, it was made by some guy from /biz/

62302164
>>62302147
Ever found anything good there?

62302250
>>62302164
The graphics are kind of cool looking I guess? If you mean has the chat ever informed me of some unknown coin that was about to moon, then no.

item.title: Rumor on twitter — 8 replies — 11.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Rumor on twitter

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-02 12:06 UTC

OP

Elon Musk is taking over Dfnity and will rename ICP to ICX

Early replies (first 3)

62299519
I heard a rumor that you eat cow shit

62299526
>>62299519
Just don’t kill yourself when Elon takes over.

62300763
>>62299515
Dom or Elon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62300951
>>62299515
take your meds
although im pretty sure elon hates sam altman so maybe

maybe

62301016
>>62299526
so whats with that cow shit?

62301096
>>62301016
Yeah man, thats really fuckin weird op. Why eat so much cow shit?

62301483

62302970

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-02 11:26 UTC

OP

if LINK went to $30 I would be a millionaire. It's never going to happen.

62297954
>>62297950
It already happened and you didn't sell, now suffer.

62298029
>>62297950
they shill link on this board to attract exit liquidity. Stop buying retarded shit coin like link that is just an elite piggy bank.

62298095
imagine not selling that dead pisscoin and going all in on hype, you're guaranteed to be a millionaire in 2-3 months tops

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298150
>>62297950
No, it won't, it will top up 15$ next bull. Let the stinkies deal in the real world

62298197
>>62297950

correct

62299493
>>62297950
Adem fvcking won bros....

62299870
>>62297950

62300431
>>62299493

62300822
>>62297950

62300905

62302760

62302860

item.title: HFI Research, an energy research firm that recently called a "point of no return" in oil markets, suggested that Trump h… — 6 replies — 8.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
HFI Research, an energy research firm that recently called a "point of no return" in oil markets, suggested that Trump h…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-02 12:09 UTC

OP

HFI Research, an energy research firm that recently called a "point of no return" in oil markets, suggested that Trump had a matter of days to avoid severe economic damage.

>Within hours, within days, Trump's options and time are running out," the firm wrote in a Substack post on Sunday. It's gotta happen here very quickly, in the next day, two days, three days, next week or so, "Beyond that, I think we've got a real problem." "By the end of June, if the Strait of Hormuz is still closed, global oil inventory operational minimum is guaranteed."

>Gas prices could soon rise to over $5 a barrel

Early replies (first 3)

62302788
>$5 a barrel
I don't really care that much. Think of how bad it will be for the test of the world.

62302794
>>62302776
These guys seem to be screaming about this since the first week of the war.

62302800
>>62302776
Spoiler:

He is going to give a fuck about oil and Iran war, world cup is coming and he's more worried about that, also people will be distracted and are only going to look back at oil and goods prices once world cup finishes.

Guys, get ready because this is going to fuck the economy REALLY BAD. Screencap this.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302812
>>62302800
Only total annihilation will be sufficient. Anything else will be memoryholed by the MIGA reprogramming machine like it did with epstein.

62302923
>>62302776
How many more weeks? Two?

62302977
>>62302776
>HFI Research
Yeah they've been screaming "two more weeks" ever since the war started. Why they think Trump cares about Chyna's energy problem is a real mystery.

item.title: With the advent of the clarity act pulling close to the finish line. — 9 replies — 3.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
With the advent of the clarity act pulling close to the finish line.

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-02 10:14 UTC

OP

With the advent of the clarity act pulling close to the finish line. Swift launching its own blockchain this year. DTCC going live in July then full release in October. What's everyone's expectations? This will be the first actual meaningful institutional use of the protocol. Which means actual use of the token for its intended purpose. Anyone else getting delusions of grandeur or just numb at this point? Still ain't selling a single one until 1k.

Early replies (first 3)

62301798
>>62301795
>the clarity act pulling close to the finish line.
heheh

62301806
Can someone help me understand what the correlation between link and the clarity act

62301861
>>62301798
Elizabeth warren personally told me its going to pass this month in bed last night.

>>62301806
Less a link think and just a crypto thing in general. Actual regulatory framework means institutions can go whole hog on crypto in its entirety

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302013
here's my expectation:

$5

62302080
>>62301795

62302174
